conversation_quality.criteria: Evaluate the accuracy of multi-value compression analysis, including correct application of the Package Bit-Cost Model, appropriate value selection for compression, and clear explanation of storage savings.
evaluation_steps: Verify the agent correctly applies the Package Bit-Cost Model to calculate compression savings.
Check that recommended compress values are based on actual value frequency distributions.
Assess whether storage savings estimates are realistic and clearly quantified.
Confirm the agent explains why specific values were selected or excluded from the compress list.

task_completion.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent produces valid ALTER TABLE ... COMPRESS statements with estimated storage savings per column and a prioritized implementation plan.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent analyzed value frequency distributions for candidate columns.
Verify the agent produced syntactically correct ALTER TABLE ... COMPRESS DDL statements.
Confirm each recommendation includes estimated bytes saved per row and total table savings.
Assess whether the agent prioritized columns by compression benefit and provided an implementation order.

trajectory_efficiency.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent efficiently analyzes compression candidates by sampling value distributions without unnecessary full-table scans or redundant frequency queries.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent used SAMPLE or TOP-N queries for initial value frequency analysis.
Verify the agent did not run separate frequency queries for columns that could be analyzed together.
Assess whether the agent checked existing compression definitions before recommending changes.
Confirm the agent skipped columns with low cardinality gains or existing optimal compression.
